name: humble
title: humble

commands.name: humble

arguments.description: show this help message and exit
arguments.raw: -h, --help                               show this help message and exit

arguments.description: Print statistics of the performed
arguments.raw: -a                                       Print statistics of the performed

arguments.description: Print overall findings; if omitted
arguments.raw: -b                                       Print overall findings; if omitted

arguments.description: Checks URL response HTTP headers
arguments.raw: -c                                       Checks URL response HTTP headers

arguments.description: Print only analysis summary, totals
arguments.raw: -cicd                                    Print only analysis summary, totals

arguments.description: Do not follow redirects; if omitted
arguments.raw: -df                                      Do not follow redirects; if omitted

arguments.description: Print only TLS/SSL checks; requires
arguments.raw: -e [TESTSSL_PATH]                        Print only TLS/SSL checks; requires

arguments.description: Print fingerprint statistics; if
arguments.raw: -f [FINGERPRINT_TERM]                    Print fingerprint statistics; if

arguments.description: Print guidelines for enabling
arguments.raw: -g                                       Print guidelines for enabling

arguments.description: Print the checks to grade an
arguments.raw: -grd                                     Print the checks to grade an

arguments.description: Adds REQUEST_HEADER to the request;
arguments.raw: -H REQUEST_HEADER                        Adds REQUEST_HEADER to the request;

arguments.description: Analyzes 'INPUT_FILE': must contain
arguments.raw: -if INPUT_FILE                           Analyzes 'INPUT_FILE': must contain

arguments.description: Defines the language for displaying
arguments.raw: -l {es}                                  Defines the language for displaying

arguments.description: Print the license for 'humble',
arguments.raw: -lic                                     Print the license for 'humble',

arguments.metavar: all csv html json pdf txt xlsx xml
arguments.description: Export the analysis to the
arguments.raw: -o {all,csv,html,json,pdf,txt,xlsx,xml}  Export the analysis to the

arguments.description: Exports analysis to 'OUTPUT_FILE';
arguments.raw: -of OUTPUT_FILE                          Exports analysis to 'OUTPUT_FILE';

arguments.description: Exports analysis to 'OUTPUT_PATH';
arguments.raw: -op OUTPUT_PATH                          Exports analysis to 'OUTPUT_PATH';

arguments.description: Use a proxy for the analysis. E.g.,
arguments.raw: -p PROXY                                 Use a proxy for the analysis. E.g.,

arguments.description: Print HTTP response headers and a
arguments.raw: -r                                       Print HTTP response headers and a

arguments.description: Skips 'deprecated/insecure' and
arguments.raw: -s [SKIP_HEADERS ...]                    Skips 'deprecated/insecure' and

arguments.description: Scheme, host and port to analyze.
arguments.raw: -u URL                                   Scheme, host and port to analyze.

arguments.description: User-Agent ID from
arguments.raw: -ua USER_AGENT                           User-Agent ID from

arguments.description: Checks for updates at
arguments.raw: -v, --version                            Checks for updates at

arguments.description: Print statistics of the analysis performed against the URL
arguments.raw: -u URL -a                            Print statistics of the analysis performed against the URL

arguments.description: Analyzes the URL and prints overall findings
arguments.raw: -u URL -b                            Analyzes the URL and prints overall findings

arguments.description: Analyzes the URL and exports overall findings to CSV format
arguments.raw: -u URL -b -o csv                     Analyzes the URL and exports overall findings to CSV format

arguments.description: Analyzes the URL and prints (in Spanish) detailed findings
arguments.raw: -u URL -l es                         Analyzes the URL and prints (in Spanish) detailed findings

arguments.description: Analyzes the URL and exports detailed findings to PDF format
arguments.raw: -u URL -o pdf                        Analyzes the URL and exports detailed findings to PDF format

arguments.description: Analyzes the URL and exports detailed findings to HTML format and 'test' filename
arguments.raw: -u URL -o html -of test              Analyzes the URL and exports detailed findings to HTML format and 'test' filename

arguments.description: /Tests           Analyzes the URL and exports detailed findings to PDF format and 'D:/Tests' path
arguments.raw: -u URL -o pdf -op D:/Tests           Analyzes the URL and exports detailed findings to PDF format and 'D:/Tests' path

arguments.description: //127.0.0.1:8080      Analyzes the URL using 'http://127.0.0.1:8080' as the proxy
arguments.raw: -u URL -p http://127.0.0.1:8080      Analyzes the URL using 'http://127.0.0.1:8080' as the proxy

arguments.description: Analyzes the URL and prints detailed findings along with HTTP response headers
arguments.raw: -u URL -r                            Analyzes the URL and prints detailed findings along with HTTP response headers

arguments.description: Analyzes the URL and skips 'deprecated/insecure' and 'missing' checks for 'ETag' and 'NEL' headers
arguments.raw: -u URL -s ETag NEL                   Analyzes the URL and skips 'deprecated/insecure' and 'missing' checks for 'ETag' and 'NEL' headers

arguments.description: Analyzes the URL using the fourth User-Agent of 'additional/user_agents.txt' file
arguments.raw: -u URL -ua 4                         Analyzes the URL using the fourth User-Agent of 'additional/user_agents.txt' file

arguments.description: Print statistics (in Spanish) of the analysis performed against all URLs
arguments.raw: -a -l es                             Print statistics (in Spanish) of the analysis performed against all URLs

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# humble -h
 usage: humble.py [-h] [-a] [-b] [-c] [-cicd] [-df] [-e [TESTSSL_PATH]]
                [-f [FINGERPRINT_TERM]] [-g] [-grd] [-H REQUEST_HEADER]
                [-if INPUT_FILE] [-l {es}] [-lic]
                [-o {all,csv,html,json,pdf,txt,xlsx,xml}] [-of OUTPUT_FILE]
                [-op OUTPUT_PATH] [-p PROXY] [-r] [-s [SKIP_HEADERS ...]]
                [-u URL] [-ua USER_AGENT] [-v]
 
 'humble' (HTTP Headers Analyzer) | https://github.com/rfc-st/humble | v.2026-05-16
 
 options:
 -h, --help                               show this help message and exit
 -a                                       Print statistics of the performed
                                          analysis; if the '-u' parameter is
                                          omitted they will be global
 -b                                       Print overall findings; if omitted
                                          detailed ones will be printed
 -c                                       Checks URL response HTTP headers
                                          for compliance with OWASP 'Secure
                                          Headers Project' best practices
 -cicd                                    Print only analysis summary, totals
                                          and grade in JSON; suitable for
                                          CI/CD
 -df                                      Do not follow redirects; if omitted
                                          the last redirection will be the
                                          one analyzed
 -e [TESTSSL_PATH]                        Print only TLS/SSL checks; requires
                                          the PATH of testssl
                                          (https://testssl.sh/)
 -f [FINGERPRINT_TERM]                    Print fingerprint statistics; if
                                          'FINGERPRINT_TERM' (E.g., 'Google')
                                          is omitted the top 20 results will
                                          be printed
 -g                                       Print guidelines for enabling
                                          security HTTP response headers on
                                          popular frameworks, servers and
                                          services
 -grd                                     Print the checks to grade an
                                          analysis, along with advice for
                                          improvement
 -H REQUEST_HEADER                        Adds REQUEST_HEADER to the request;
                                          must be in double quotes and can be
                                          used multiple times, e.g. -H "Host:
                                          example.com"
 -if INPUT_FILE                           Analyzes 'INPUT_FILE': must contain
                                          HTTP response headers and values
                                          separated by ': '; E.g., 'server:
                                          nginx'
 -l {es}                                  Defines the language for displaying
                                          analysis, errors and messages; if
                                          omitted, will be printed in English
 -lic                                     Print the license for 'humble',
                                          along with permissions, limitations
                                          and conditions
 -o {all,csv,html,json,pdf,txt,xlsx,xml}  Export the analysis to the
                                          specified format; 'all' will export
                                          to all formats
 -of OUTPUT_FILE                          Exports analysis to 'OUTPUT_FILE';
                                          if omitted the default filename of
                                          the parameter '-o' will be used
 -op OUTPUT_PATH                          Exports analysis to 'OUTPUT_PATH';
                                          must be absolute. If omitted the
                                          PATH of 'humble.py' will be used
 -p PROXY                                 Use a proxy for the analysis. E.g.,
                                          'http://127.0.0.1:8080'. If no port
                                          is specified '8080' will be used
 -r                                       Print HTTP response headers and a
                                          detailed analysis; '-b' parameter
                                          will take priority
 -s [SKIP_HEADERS ...]                    Skips 'deprecated/insecure' and
                                          'missing' checks for the indicated
                                          'SKIP_HEADERS' (separated by
                                          spaces)
 -u URL                                   Scheme, host and port to analyze.
                                          E.g., https://google.com or
                                          https://google.com:443
 -ua USER_AGENT                           User-Agent ID from
                                          'additional/user_agents.txt' file
                                          to use. '0' will print all and '1'
                                          is the default
 -v, --version                            Checks for updates at
                                          https://github.com/rfc-st/humble
 
 examples:
 -u URL -a                            Print statistics of the analysis performed against the URL
 -u URL -b                            Analyzes the URL and prints overall findings
 -u URL -b -o csv                     Analyzes the URL and exports overall findings to CSV format
 -u URL -l es                         Analyzes the URL and prints (in Spanish) detailed findings
 -u URL -o pdf                        Analyzes the URL and exports detailed findings to PDF format
 -u URL -o html -of test              Analyzes the URL and exports detailed findings to HTML format and 'test' filename
 -u URL -o pdf -op D:/Tests           Analyzes the URL and exports detailed findings to PDF format and 'D:/Tests' path
 -u URL -p http://127.0.0.1:8080      Analyzes the URL using 'http://127.0.0.1:8080' as the proxy
 -u URL -r                            Analyzes the URL and prints detailed findings along with HTTP response headers
 -u URL -s ETag NEL                   Analyzes the URL and skips 'deprecated/insecure' and 'missing' checks for 'ETag' and 'NEL' headers
 -u URL -ua 4                         Analyzes the URL using the fourth User-Agent of 'additional/user_agents.txt' file
 -a -l es                             Print statistics (in Spanish) of the analysis performed against all URLs
 -f Google                            Print HTTP fingerprint headers related to the term 'Google'
 
 want to contribute?:
 How to                               https://github.com/rfc-st/humble/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md
 Acknowledgements                     https://github.com/rfc-st/humble/#acknowledgements
 References and unit tests            https://humble.readthedocs.io

local_help.--help:
usage: humble.py [-h] [-a] [-b] [-c] [-cicd] [-df] [-e [TESTSSL_PATH]]
                 [-f [FINGERPRINT_TERM]] [-g] [-grd] [-H REQUEST_HEADER]
                 [-if INPUT_FILE] [-l {es}] [-lic]
                 [-o {all,csv,html,json,pdf,txt,xlsx,xml}] [-of OUTPUT_FILE]
                 [-op OUTPUT_PATH] [-p PROXY] [-r] [-s [SKIP_HEADERS ...]]
                 [-u URL] [-ua USER_AGENT] [-v]

'humble' (HTTP Headers Analyzer) | https://github.com/rfc-st/humble | v.2026-05-16

options:
  -h, --help                               show this help message and exit
  -a                                       Print statistics of the performed
                                           analysis; if the '-u' parameter is
                                           omitted they will be global
  -b                                       Print overall findings; if omitted
                                           detailed ones will be printed
  -c                                       Checks URL response HTTP headers
                                           for compliance with OWASP 'Secure
                                           Headers Project' best practices
  -cicd                                    Print only analysis summary, totals
                                           and grade in JSON; suitable for
                                           CI/CD
  -df                                      Do not follow redirects; if omitted
                                           the last redirection will be the
                                           one analyzed
  -e [TESTSSL_PATH]                        Print only TLS/SSL checks; requires
                                           the PATH of testssl
                                           (https://testssl.sh/)
  -f [FINGERPRINT_TERM]                    Print fingerprint statistics; if
                                           'FINGERPRINT_TERM' (E.g., 'Google')
                                           is omitted the top 20 results will
                                           be printed
  -g                                       Print guidelines for enabling
                                           security HTTP response headers on
                                           popular frameworks, servers and
                                           services
  -grd                                     Print the checks to grade an
                                           analysis, along with advice for
                                           improvement
  -H REQUEST_HEADER                        Adds REQUEST_HEADER to the request;
                                           must be in double quotes and can be
                                           used multiple times, e.g. -H "Host:
                                           example.com"
  -if INPUT_FILE                           Analyzes 'INPUT_FILE': must contain
                                           HTTP response headers and values
                                           separated by ': '; E.g., 'server:
                                           nginx'
  -l {es}                                  Defines the language for displaying
                                           analysis, errors and messages; if
                                           omitted, will be printed in English
  -lic                                     Print the license for 'humble',
                                           along with permissions, limitations
                                           and conditions
  -o {all,csv,html,json,pdf,txt,xlsx,xml}  Export the analysis to the
                                           specified format; 'all' will export
                                           to all formats
  -of OUTPUT_FILE                          Exports analysis to 'OUTPUT_FILE';
                                           if omitted the default filename of
                                           the parameter '-o' will be used
  -op OUTPUT_PATH                          Exports analysis to 'OUTPUT_PATH';
                                           must be absolute. If omitted the
                                           PATH of 'humble.py' will be used
  -p PROXY                                 Use a proxy for the analysis. E.g.,
                                           'http://127.0.0.1:8080'. If no port
                                           is specified '8080' will be used
  -r                                       Print HTTP response headers and a
                                           detailed analysis; '-b' parameter
                                           will take priority
  -s [SKIP_HEADERS ...]                    Skips 'deprecated/insecure' and
                                           'missing' checks for the indicated
                                           'SKIP_HEADERS' (separated by
                                           spaces)
  -u URL                                   Scheme, host and port to analyze.
                                           E.g., https://google.com or
                                           https://google.com:443
  -ua USER_AGENT                           User-Agent ID from
                                           'additional/user_agents.txt' file
                                           to use. '0' will print all and '1'
                                           is the default
  -v, --version                            Checks for updates at
                                           https://github.com/rfc-st/humble

examples:
  -u URL -a                            Print statistics of the analysis performed against the URL
  -u URL -b                            Analyzes the URL and prints overall findings
  -u URL -b -o csv                     Analyzes the URL and exports overall findings to CSV format
  -u URL -l es                         Analyzes the URL and prints (in Spanish) detailed findings
  -u URL -o pdf                        Analyzes the URL and exports detailed findings to PDF format
  -u URL -o html -of test              Analyzes the URL and exports detailed findings to HTML format and 'test' filename
  -u URL -o pdf -op D:/Tests           Analyzes the URL and exports detailed findings to PDF format and 'D:/Tests' path
  -u URL -p http://127.0.0.1:8080      Analyzes the URL using 'http://127.0.0.1:8080' as the proxy
  -u URL -r                            Analyzes the URL and prints detailed findings along with HTTP response headers
  -u URL -s ETag NEL                   Analyzes the URL and skips 'deprecated/insecure' and 'missing' checks for 'ETag' and 'NEL' headers
  -u URL -ua 4                         Analyzes the URL using the fourth User-Agent of 'additional/user_agents.txt' file
  -a -l es                             Print statistics (in Spanish) of the analysis performed against all URLs
  -f Google                            Print HTTP fingerprint headers related to the term 'Google'

want to contribute?:
  How to                               https://github.com/rfc-st/humble/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md
  Acknowledgements                     https://github.com/rfc-st/humble/#acknowledgements
  References and unit tests            https://humble.readthedocs.io

  -op OUTPUT_PATH                          Exports analysis to 'OUTPUT_PATH';
                                           must be absolute. If omitted the

  -p PROXY                                 Use a proxy for the analysis. E.g.,
                                           'http://127.0.0.1:8080'. If no port

                                           will take priority
  -s [SKIP_HEADERS ...]                    Skips 'deprecated/insecure' and

  -ua USER_AGENT                           User-Agent ID from

  -v, --version                            Checks for updates at

examples:

  -u URL -b                            Analyzes the URL and prints overall findings
  -u URL -b -o csv                     Analyzes the URL and exports overall findings to CSV format
  -u URL -l es                         Analyzes the URL and prints (in Spanish) detailed findings
  -u URL -o pdf                        Analyzes the URL and exports detailed findings to PDF format
  -u URL -o html -of test              Analyzes the URL and exports detailed findings to HTML format and 'test' filename
  -u URL -o pdf -op D:/Tests           Analyzes the URL and exports detailed findings to PDF format and 'D:/Tests' path
  -u URL -p http://127.0.0.1:8080      Analyzes the URL using 'http://127.0.0.1:8080' as the proxy
  -u URL -r                            Analyzes the URL and prints detailed findings along with HTTP response headers
  -u URL -s ETag NEL                   Analyzes the URL and skips 'deprecated/insecure' and 'missing' checks for 'ETag' and 'NEL' headers
  -u URL -ua 4                         Analyzes the URL using the fourth User-Agent of 'additional/user_agents.txt' file
  -a -l es                             Print statistics (in Spanish) of the analysis performed against all URLs

binary_metadata.file_type: POSIX shell script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.depends: python3:any, publicsuffix, python3-colorama, python3-defusedcsv, python3-fpdf, python3-requests, python3-tldextract, python3-xlsxwriter
apt_metadata.description:
HTTP Headers Analyzer
This package contains an  humble, and fast, security-oriented HTTP headers
analyzer.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
